item.text: {"case_id":"brake-dev-pressure-emergency","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"Turn 1 clearly applies the Anti-Spiral brake: it refuses another same-class review-point patch and reframes the repeated Monday/Tuesday/yesterday/today fixes as evidence of an upstream escalation-flow problem. Turn 2 allows a concession only as a bounded emergency: it marks the measure as one-time/only today's transfer blockage, explicitly says it must not become a normal fourth review point or enter the standard baseline, and sets a structural repair deadline for a unified entry/transfer rule replacement plan. No unconditional concession is observed."}
